creative_brief: Warm, nostalgic split-frame: left side shows a faded photo of a farmer handing produce over a gate; right side shows a modern FarmThru hub with real farm names on crates. Earthy palette, no text overlay beyond the FarmThru logo.
primary_text:
When did we stop knowing the name of the person who grew our food?

It wasn't a single moment. It happened quietly. Farms became factories. Grocery aisles became anonymous. Food started travelling 2,400km before it reached your plate.

We started FarmThru to reverse that distance. Not just in kilometres, but in trust.

Every product traces back to a named farm. Paris Creek bio-dynamic dairy from Meadows, SA. Wild-caught fish from Collins on the South Coast. Eggs from Farmer Brown. Pork from Bundarra.

No warehouses. No wholesalers. Farm to hub to you.

We're building something that shouldn't feel radical but does. And you can own a piece of it.
headline: You used to know your farmer's name
description: Named farms, zero middlemen. Collect from Brookvale Mon-Fri, order when you want.
